comparison_to_iteration_1.notes: With-skill dropped 1 assertion on eval 3 (lint hook exits 0 always instead of using exit 2 for unfixable errors — the skill says 'report but don't block' which is a reasonable design choice but the grader judged strictly). Baseline dropped further on evals 1&2 due to new engineering standards assertions which baseline cannot satisfy.

notes: Eval 1 (Bootstrap): with_skill 10/10, baseline 6/10 (+40%). The new Engineering Standards section is the key differentiator — with_skill produced opinionated guidance on component patterns, state management (decision tree), API fetching, Zod validation, and testing philosophy with explicit anti-patterns.
Eval 2 (Diagnose): with_skill 8/8, baseline 4/8 (+50%). Largest delta. Baseline still lacks pillar framework, formal audit report, and now also lacks engineering standards in fixed-CLAUDE.md.

Eval 3 (Hooks): with_skill 7/8, baseline 8/8 (-12.5%). Baseline actually scored higher this time — it used exit 2 for unfixable lint errors while with_skill always exits 0 (report-only). Both got the settings.json format correct this time.
The engineering standards feedback from iteration 1 is working — with_skill now produces rich domain guidance that baseline cannot match.
